sections.paragraph:
καρποφορέω (s. καρπός, φέρω) fut. καρποφορήσω; 1 aor. ἐκαρποφόρησα

① to cause production of fruit or seeds, bear fruit/crops lit. (X. et al.; Theophr., HP 3, 3, 7; Diod S 2, 49, 4; PMichZen106, 6 [III b.c.]; Wsd 10:7; Hab 3:17; En 5:1; Just., D. 110, 4) of land Mk 4:28 (γῆ κ. as Jos., C. Ap. 1, 306; Did., Gen. 31, 14). Of a vine Hs 2:3.

② to cause the inner life to be productive, bear fruit (Philo, Cher. 84 κ. ἀρετάς; Did., Gen. 32, 24; for construction w. acc. cp. Hippol., Ref. 6, 46, 3; abs. OdeSol 11:23; cp. SEG XLII, 1193, 8 and 1421, 4 both Christian V a.d.) Mt 13:23; Mk 4:20; Lk 8:15; Hs 4:5, 8. κ. ἐν τῇ καρδίᾳ bear fruit in the heart, i.e. in a resolve to do what is right B 11:11; cp. ἐν σεαυτῷ Hs 4:5. Of faith τῆς πίστεως ῥίζα καρποφορεῖ εἰς τὸν κύριον ἡμῶν Ἰησοῦν Χριστόν the root of faith yields fruit in (or to) our Lord Jesus Christ Pol 1:2. W. dat. of advantage κ. τῷ θεῷ Ro 7:4 (so OdeSol 11:1). Also τῷ θανάτῳ vs. 5. κ. ἐν παντὶ ἔργῳ ἀγαθῷ bear fruit in all kinds of good deeds Col 1:10.—Mid. (IBM 918) bear fruit of itself 1:6.—DELG s.v. φέρω p. 1190. M-M. TW.

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Theophr Theophr , IV–III b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

En En = I Enoch—List 2

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Did. Did. = Didymus Caecus (the Blind), commentator on OT books, IV a.d.—List 5

Hs Hs = Similitudes

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

acc. acc. = accusative

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Hippol Hippol , II–III a.d.—List 5

abs. abs. = absolute

OdeSol OdeSol = Gk. Ode of Solomon 11: PBodmer XI; III a.d.—List 2

SEG SEG = Supplementum Epigraphicum Graecum—List 3

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

Pol Pol = Polycarp to the Philippians—List 1

dat. dat. = dative

IBM IBM = Ancient Greek Inscriptions in the British Museum—List 3

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 510
